yamlDiagnostics.acceptedValuesMissingConfig: Column "{{column}}": accepted_values is missing its configuration block — check indentation.
yamlDiagnostics.acceptedValuesWrongIndent: Column "{{column}}": values: must be indented inside arguments:, not at the same level.
yamlDiagnostics.relationshipsMissingConfig: Column "{{column}}": relationships is missing its configuration block — check indentation.
yamlDiagnostics.relationshipsWrongIndent: Column "{{column}}": to: and field: must be indented inside arguments:, not at the same level.

lessonPanel.courseComplete: You've finished the course. Go build something with dbt.

intro.tagline: Learn dbt free in your browser.
intro.subTagline: {{count}} short interactive lessons. Real SQL, a real warehouse, a real DAG. All running in this page. No install, no signup.
intro.ctaBeginFull: Begin Lesson 1: Your first dbt model ›
intro.restartConfirm: Start the course over from Lesson 1? Your task progress will be cleared.
intro.welcomeMid: , a series of short, interactive lessons designed to help you learn
intro.welcomeTail: right in your browser, inspired by

whatIsDbt.heading: What is dbt?
whatIsDbt.body: dbt is the open-source tool data teams use to turn raw warehouse tables into trustworthy, documented, tested models. It sits on top of any SQL warehouse (Snowflake, Databricks, BigQuery, Postgres, DuckDB, …) and gives you a way to manage your transformation SQL *as code*: version-controlled, modular, and testable.

didYouKnow.title: Did you know?
didYouKnow.body: dbt projects are just folders of `.sql` and `.yml` files. No proprietary syntax. If you can write SQL, you can write dbt. But dbt turns those files into something bigger: a managed, tested, documented, version-controlled transformation framework with full lineage.

dbtRun.intro: A dbt project is a folder full of `.sql` files. Each file is a `SELECT` statement (what dbt calls a **model**). When you run `dbt run`, dbt:
dbtRun.step2: Figures out which models depend on others (the **DAG**)
dbtRun.step3: Runs them in the right order and builds the results in your Database
dbtRun.outro: That's the core idea. Everything else (tests, docs, sources, materializations, snapshots) is built on top of those primitives.

exampleDag.heading: An example DAG
exampleDag.intro: Real pipelines have several layers. A common pattern looks like this:
exampleDag.caption: The arrows are `ref()` calls in SQL. dbt reads them, builds the graph above, and runs the models left-to-right.

aboutLessons.heading: About the lessons
aboutLessons.p1: There are {{count}} short lessons. Each one introduces a single concept, then gives you 3–5 small tasks to apply it. Your progress saves automatically — close the tab and come back later.
aboutLessons.p2: Go at your pace, edit the SQL freely, and don't worry about breaking things. Every lesson has a "Reset lesson" button in the top bar. If you get stuck, every task has a "Show hint" button.
aboutLessons.p3: By the end you'll be ready to open any real dbt project and contribute on day one.

beforeYouStart.heading: Before you start
beforeYouStart.body: You only need to know basic SQL — `SELECT`, `WHERE`, `GROUP BY`. That's it. No Database, no dbt installation, no command-line experience needed. Everything runs in your browser.

files.title: Files
files.body: The dbt project folder. Models live under models/, tests under tests/, raw CSV seeds under seeds/. Click a file to open it in the editor.

editor.title: Editor
editor.body: Where you write SQL and YAML. Edits save instantly — there is no separate "save" step.

terminal.title: Terminal / Results / Lineage
terminal.body: Run dbt commands (dbt run, dbt test, dbt build), inspect query results, and watch the DAG update as you add ref() calls between models.

lesson.title: Lesson panel
lesson.body: You are here. Each lesson opens with a short concept, then a checklist of tasks. The 'Show hint' button is your friend.

courseComplete.title: You finished dbt-quest 🎉
courseComplete.body: You can read any real dbt project now: sources, models, refs, materializations, seeds, tests, docs, selectors, `dbt build`. That's the toolkit.
courseComplete.step2Lead: Open an existing project on GitHub (try
courseComplete.step3Lead: Skim the official docs you didn't see here:

terminal.emptyHintLead: type a dbt command below - try

dag.emptyHint: add a model to see the graph

errorBoundary.title: dbt-quest hit an unexpected error
errorBoundary.body: Try reloading the page. If the problem persists, you can reset your saved progress — this will clear completed lessons and unlocked panels, but should recover the app.

privacy.title: Privacy Policy

introduction.heading: 1. Introduction
introduction.p1: Welcome to dbt quest. We respect your privacy and are committed to being transparent about how we collect and use information about you.
introduction.p2: This Privacy Policy describes how we collect, use, and protect your data when you use our website.

dataCollection.heading: 2. Data Collection
dataCollection.intro: We collect the following types of information:
dataCollection.navData: Navigation Data: Information about how you use our website, such as pages visited, time spent, and actions performed. This data is collected in aggregate and anonymously.
dataCollection.browserData: Browser Data: Browser type, operating system, language, and similar technical information.
dataCollection.localStorage: Local Storage: Your lesson progress is saved locally in your browser to improve the learning experience.

dataUsage.heading: 3. How We Use Your Data
dataUsage.intro: We use the collected data to:
dataUsage.improve: Improve and optimize the user experience
dataUsage.understand: Understand how you interact with our content
dataUsage.analyze: Analyze usage trends on the website

cloudflare.heading: 4. Cloudflare Web Analytics
cloudflare.intro: We use Cloudflare Web Analytics to collect analytics data about how visitors use our website. This tool:
cloudflare.aggregate: Collects data in aggregate and anonymously
cloudflare.noCookies: Does not use third-party tracking cookies
cloudflare.noIndividual: Does not identify you as a specific individual
cloudflare.dnt: Respects the "Do Not Track" setting in your browser

storage.heading: 5. Data Storage
storage.p1: Your progress is stored only in your browser's local storage. We do not save any personal information on our servers.
storage.p2: If you clear your browser data, your progress will be lost. We recommend not doing this during your studies.

rights.heading: 6. Your Rights
rights.intro: Depending on your location, you may have rights regarding your data:
rights.gdpr: GDPR (EU): Right to access, rectification, deletion, and data portability
rights.lgpd: LGPD (Brazil): Right to access, correction, deletion, and opposition to processing

contact.heading: 7. Contact
contact.text: If you have questions about this Privacy Policy or how we handle your data, please open an issue on

privacy.footer: We reserve the right to update this Privacy Policy at any time. Significant changes will be communicated here.
